description: SlackAction configures a single Slack action that is sent with each notification. See https://api.slack.com/docs/message-attachments#action_fields and https://api.slack.com/docs/message-buttons for more information.

confirm.description: confirm defines an optional confirmation dialog that appears before the action is executed. When set, users must confirm their intent before the action proceeds.

name.description: name defines a unique identifier for the action within the message. This value is sent back to your application when the action is triggered.

style.description: style defines the visual appearance of the action element. Valid values include "default", "primary" (green), and "danger" (red).

text.description: text defines the user-visible label displayed on the action element. For buttons, this is the button text. For select menus, this is the placeholder text.

type.description: type defines the type of interactive component. Common values include "button" for clickable buttons and "select" for dropdown menus.

url.description: url defines the URL to open when the action is triggered. Only applicable for button-type actions. When set, clicking the button opens this URL.

value.description: value defines the payload sent when the action is triggered. This data is included in the callback sent to your application.
